examples.title: Basic Network Security Communication Model
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the basic model of secure message transmission including sender, encryption, channel, and receiver
examples.explanation: This code shows the fundamental process: sender encrypts a message using a shared key, transmits it through a potentially insecure channel, and the receiver decrypts it. The secure channel represents the protection mechanism.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is message length

examples.title: Attack Detection Simulation
examples.description: Pseudocode simulating detection of passive and active attacks on network communications
examples.explanation: This code simulates monitoring network traffic to detect different attack types. It checks for signs of passive attacks (unexpected observers) and active attacks (data modification, service disruption).
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Security Monitoring System
 
 TYPE AttackType = {NONE, PASSIVE, ACTIVE}
 
 FUNCTION monitorNetworkTraffic(packetData):
 
 // Check for passive attack indicators
 IF isUnexpectedObserverDetected():
 RETURN AttackType.PASSIVE // Eavesdropping detected
 
 IF isTrafficPatternAnomalous(packetData):
 RETURN AttackType.PASSIVE // Traffic analysis detected
 
 // Check for active attack indicators
 IF isMessageModified(packetData):
 RETURN AttackType.ACTIVE // Modification attack
 
 IF isMasqueradeDetected(packetData):
 RETURN AttackType.ACTIVE // Masquerade attack
 
 IF isReplayDetected(packetData):
 RETURN AttackType.ACTIVE // Replay attack
 
 IF isServiceDisrupted():
 RETURN AttackType.ACTIVE // DoS attack
 
 RETURN AttackType.NONE
 
 FUNCTION respondToAttack(attackType):
 IF attackType == AttackType.PASSIVE:
 PRINT "Alert: Passive attack detected - monitoring continues"
 // Log for analysis, enhance encryption
 
 IF attackType == AttackType.ACTIVE:
 PRINT "Alert: Active attack detected - taking countermeasures"
 // Block source, alert admin, terminate session
 
 // Main monitoring loop
 WHILE systemRunning:
 packet = captureNetworkPacket()
 attack = monitorNetworkTraffic(packet)
 IF attack != AttackType.NONE:
 respondToAttack(attack)
END

examples.title: Security Services Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating implementation of core security services: confidentiality, integrity, authentication, and non-repudiation
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how multiple security services work together. Encryption provides confidentiality, hashing ensures integrity, digital signatures provide authentication and non-repudiation.
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Security Services Implementation
 
 RECORD SecureMessage:
 encryptedContent // For confidentiality
 hashValue // For integrity
 digitalSignature // For authentication & non-repudiation
 senderCertificate // For verifying sender identity
 
 FUNCTION provideConfidentiality(message, key):
 // Encrypt message to hide content
 encrypted = AES_ENCRYPT(message, key)
 RETURN encrypted
 
 FUNCTION provideIntegrity(message):
 // Generate hash to detect modification
 hashValue = SHA256(message)
 RETURN hashValue
 
 FUNCTION provideAuthentication(message, privateKey):
 // Sign message with sender's private key
 signature = RSA_SIGN(message, privateKey)
 RETURN signature
 
 FUNCTION provideNonRepudiation(message, signature, publicKey):
 // Verify signature to prove origin
 isValid = RSA_VERIFY(message, signature, publicKey)
 IF isValid:
 PRINT "Sender cannot deny sending this message"
 RETURN isValid
 
 FUNCTION sendSecureMessage(message, senderPrivateKey, receiverPublicKey):
 // Step 1: Provide confidentiality
 encryptedMsg = provideConfidentiality(message, receiverPublicKey)
 
 // Step 2: Provide integrity
 integrityHash = provideIntegrity(message)
 
 // Step 3: Provide authentication and non-repudiation
 signature = provideAuthentication(message, senderPrivateKey)
 
 // Create complete secure message
 secureMessage = SecureMessage(
 encryptedContent: encryptedMsg,
 hashValue: integrityHash,
 digitalSignature: signature,
 senderCertificate: getCertificate(senderPrivateKey)
 )
 
 RETURN secureMessage
 
 FUNCTION receiveSecureMessage(secureMessage, senderPublicKey, privateKey):
 // Step 1: Decrypt to get original message
 decryptedMsg = DECRYPT(secureMessage.encryptedContent, privateKey)
 
 // Step 2: Verify integrity
 computedHash = SHA256(decryptedMsg)
 IF computedHash != secureMessage.hashValue:
 PRINT "Integrity check failed - message modified!"
 RETURN null
 
 // Step 3: Verify authentication and non-repudiation
 isAuthentic = provideNonRepudiation(
 decryptedMsg, 
 secureMessage.digitalSignature, 
 senderPublicKey
 )
 
 IF isAuthentic:
 PRINT "Message verified and authenticated"
 RETURN decryptedMsg
 ELSE:
 PRINT "Authentication failed"
 RETURN null
END
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for message processing, O(k) for signature verification
